_comment: Metatile legend for LAYOUT_ROUTE1 (Tidal Reach coastal route east of Zeegem). Primary tileset gTileset_General, secondary gTileset_Dewford (same pair as LAYOUT_ZEEGEM). Every entry below was copied from a real, working map already in this repo -- IDs, collision, and elevation are not invented. Sources: data/layouts/Zeegem/map.bin (shoreline + rocks + solo trees + tree wall), data/layouts/Route101/map.bin (tall grass, ledge, plain grass -- General primary tileset is shared across every vanilla route so these tiles work unmodified here). collision: 0 = walkable, 1 = blocked (per pokeemerald map.bin bit 10-11 encoding). elevation: bits 12-15, 3 matches Zeegem's ground level, 0 matches Zeegem's water level.

CLEARING.source: Zeegem main plaza ground (SandPit_Center graphic), reused as rest-spot clearing accent

TREE_EDGE.source: Zeegem border, inner transition tile next to open ground

TREE_SOLO.source: Zeegem standalone landmark tree (collision forced to 1 here; Zeegem's own instance is walkable, likely because it's boxed in by neighboring tree-wall tiles there, but a lone tree candidate in open Route1 grass must block)

ledge_jump_south._comment: One-way hop south. Verbatim 5-wide unit from Route101 rows 6-7 (06e cap / 087 x3 hop tiles / 0d6 cap). Extend the middle run for a wider ledge; caps stay single-width at each end.

shore_and_sea._comment: Two-level coastline copied tile-for-tile from Zeegem's own south shore (rows 16-19). Baseline coastline: land / SHORE_FILL row / SEA rows. A cove is formed by shifting the SHORE_FILL row one row further north for a column span, bridged by the corner pair below.

SHORE_CORNER_L.note: west end of a shore-edge run; land sits to its west and north-west

SHORE_FILL.note: straight shore-edge run, tiles freely with itself

SHORE_CORNER_R.note: east end of a shore-edge run; land sits to its east and north-east

SHORE_STEP_L.note: sits directly under SHORE_CORNER_L, bridges edge row down to open sea

SEA.note: open deep water, tiles freely with itself in all directions

border_fill._comment: border.bin (repeats infinitely beyond the map edges). Zeegem's own border.bin is 4x SEA (0x170) since its south edge is coastline -- Route1 reuses the same so the horizon beyond the sea reads as more open water.
